gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: Reentrancy in swapExactInput during DEX migration via luanchToDEX external call before _setTokenStatus(DEX) write
candidate.claimed_root_cause: No reentrancy guard anywhere in Portal/PortalBase/PortalCommon; if the PORTAL_TRADE_V2 implementation makes the external luanchToDEX call before writing TokenStatus.DEX to storage, a re-entrant swapExactInput sees status==Tradable and buys at bonding-curve price after migration is triggered
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker acquires tokens at bonding-curve price that should have been locked into the DEX pool, draining migration liquidity

live_preconditions.name: Tradable token with circulatingSupply near dexSupplyThresh
live_preconditions.observed_value: unknown — _packedTokenStates mapping not enumerable without known keys
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: cast call 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 'getTokenV8Safe(address)' <token> --rpc-url $BSC_RPC | check circulatingSupply vs dexSupplyThresh; enumerate candidate tokens from recent TokenBought events near LaunchedToDEX events

live_preconditions.observed_value: unknown — immutable set in constructor, not readable from indexed source

live_preconditions.name: PORTAL_TRADE_V2 source code — ordering of _setTokenStatus(DEX) vs luanchToDEX external call
live_preconditions.observed_value: unknown — PORTAL_TRADE_V2 contract is not among verified source files in Kirstennew/Portal--001; only Portal.sol, PortalBase.sol, PortalCommon.sol, and interfaces are indexed
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: resolve PORTAL_TRADE_V2 address from deployment, fetch verified source from BSCScan, grep for _setTokenStatus and luanchToDEX call ordering in the buy/migration path

live_preconditions.name: No reentrancy guard in Portal/PortalBase/PortalCommon
live_preconditions.observed_value: CONFIRMED — exhaustive grep for nonReentrant, ReentrancyGuard, _status, _locked, reentrancy in src/ returns zero matches in Portal.sol, PortalBase.sol, PortalCommon.sol

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Entry point delegates entirely to PORTAL_TRADE_V2 via _delegateToImpl; no reentrancy guard at this layer

Routes to PORTAL_TRADE_V2 when status==Tradable; routes to PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER when status==DEX. A re-entrant call during migration would see status==Tradable if _setTokenStatus(DEX) has not yet been written, routing back to PORTAL_TRADE_V2 buy logic
Writes TokenStatus to _packedTokenStates storage slot. If this write occurs after the external luanchToDEX call in PORTAL_TRADE_V2, the reentrancy window is open
External call in migration path; NatSpec confirms it may be delegatecalled from a payable function, confirming an external call exists in the migration path

attacker_inputs: ExactInputParams with inputToken=address(0) (native BNB) or malicious ERC-20 quote token, outputToken=target Tradable token, inputAmount sized to push circulatingSupply >= dexSupplyThresh
call_sequence: 1. Attacker calls swapExactInput(params) on Portal proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0
3. PORTAL_TRADE_V2 buy logic mints tokens, updates circulatingSupply, detects circulatingSupply >= dexSupplyThresh, enters migration path
4. IF PORTAL_TRADE_V2 calls luanchToDEX (external) BEFORE calling _setTokenStatus(DEX): attacker's contract receives ETH/ERC-20 callback and re-enters swapExactInput
5. Re-entrant call: _getSwapImplementation reads status==Tradable (not yet updated), routes to PORTAL_TRADE_V2 again, buys additional tokens at bonding-curve price
6. Outer call resumes, calls _setTokenStatus(DEX), migration completes with reduced reserve
state_before: Portal proxy holds BNB reserve for the token
state_after: IF vulnerable: attacker holds tokens bought at bonding-curve price after migration triggered; DEX pool receives less liquidity than full bonding-curve reserve
IF not vulnerable (CEI holds): re-entrant call reverts with TokenNotTradable or TokenAlreadyDEXed

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: Launched tokens and/or BNB reserve that should have been deposited into the DEX liquidity pool
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Tokens acquired at bonding-curve price after migration threshold is crossed, which should be impossible post-migration
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: DEX pool receives less BNB/token liquidity than the full bonding-curve reserve; token holders and protocol lose migration proceeds
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Once circulatingSupply >= dexSupplyThresh, no further bonding-curve buys are entitled; all remaining reserve belongs to the DEX pool. A re-entrant buy at bonding-curve price extracts value that belongs to the DEX pool

setup: 1. Fork BSC at block 108382650; use proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0
2. Resolve PORTAL_TRADE_V2 address from implementation constructor args or storage; fetch and review its source to determine _setTokenStatus vs luanchToDEX ordering
3. Identify a live Tradable token with circulatingSupply near dexSupplyThresh by querying recent TokenBought/LaunchedToDEX events
4. Deploy MaliciousMigrator: when luanchToDEX is called, re-enters swapExactInput on Portal for the same token with a small BNB amount
5. If migrator address is not admin-settable, use a mock ERC-20 quote token with a transfer hook that re-enters Portal, or instrument PORTAL_TRADE_V2 locally to intercept the external call
transaction_sequence: attacker.swapExactInput({inputToken: address(0), outputToken: targetToken, inputAmount: thresholdAmount})
→ MaliciousMigrator.luanchToDEX re-enters: attacker.swapExactInput({inputToken: address(0), outputToken: targetToken, inputAmount: smallAmount})
→ check: does re-entrant call succeed or revert?
expected_assertions: If reentrancy is possible: assert re-entrant swapExactInput succeeds and attacker token balance increases; assert DEX pool reserve is less than expected full bonding-curve reserve → PROMOTE to HIGH_CONFIDENCE_CANDIDATE
If re-entrant call reverts with TokenNotTradable or TokenAlreadyDEXed: _setTokenStatus(DEX) precedes external call, CEI holds → REJECT
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: Re-entrant call reverts with TokenNotTradable or TokenAlreadyDEXed, confirming status is written before the external call

rejection_reason: Not rejected. The absence of any reentrancy guard in Portal/PortalBase/PortalCommon is confirmed. The attack plausibility depends entirely on the ordering of _setTokenStatus(DEX) vs the external luanchToDEX call inside PORTAL_TRADE_V2, which is not among the indexed source files and cannot be resolved without local fork testing or direct source review of the PORTAL_TRADE_V2 implementation contract.
